title: Make ledger resolution explicit and support shared worktree coordination
description: Stop treating an unrelated directory as an empty Waystation project and add an explicit shared-ledger operating mode for agents in separate Git worktrees. Resolve the ledger by explicit root, environment override, then upward discovery. Keep ledger storage/locking separate from the invoking agent's Git branch and worktree context so a shared claim records the real caller.
acceptance: Every non-init operation returns a ledger_not_found diagnostic when no .waystation directory can be resolved.
Ledger selection precedence is explicit --root, WAYSTATION_ROOT, then upward discovery from the caller directory.
CLI, MCP, and dashboard startup paths use the same resolver and disclose the selected ledger root.
A claim written from worktree B into a ledger rooted elsewhere records worktree B and its branch while locking and writing only the selected ledger.
A concurrent two-worktree test targeting one ledger proves exactly one same-task claim succeeds.
ADR-0003 and user documentation distinguish checkout-local default behavior from opt-in shared-ledger coordination.
notes: This task runs before mutation recovery and lifecycle expansion so later core work receives a stable ledger-root/caller-context contract. Do not automatically discover or mutate the main worktree ledger; keep shared coordination explicit and local-first.
